dialogos.texto_en: Yotsuba, I'm here.
Okaaay.\nSorry to keep you waiting.\nI'll finish changing soon, so please wait there.
It looks like they've arrived.
Good grief, what are they plotting?\nThey haven't changed since high school.\nThey're all troublemakers.
Is that including me?
You were the biggest troublemaker.
(Come to think of it, I had a dream this morning.)
(How many times have I dreamt of that day?\n It's etched so deeply in my mind.)
(It was such a terrible nightmare.)
Sorry to keep you waiting.
Am I still dreaming?
It's the final quintuplets game.
If it's true love, you can tell us apart, right?
Ladies and gentlemen, thank you for taking time\nout of your busy schedules\nto attend our wedding reception today.
I am very happy to finally have our wedding ceremony\nin front of everyone who has supported us so far.
Because, you see, the proposal itself\nhad taken place five years ago.
It...really has been a long road.
The quintuplets game...
What a joke! Except for Yotsuba, you other four!
What are you doing, wearing wedding dresses\nfor this kind of game?\nDo you even know what you're doing?
Sure, it's a bit complicated...
But we just thought of it, you know.
And it's not just for fun.
We're also relatives of the bride.
Well, that's a lie.\nI'm the bride.
No. It's me.
I'm the bride!
You can tell, right, Futaro?
Geez, you idiots...\nJust when I thought\nyou'd become somewhat sensible adults.
It's a relief you still haven't changed.\nBut don't underestimate me.
It's you.
It seems the party is at its peak,\nbut it looks like it's time to wrap things up.
Lastly, a message of gratitude\nfrom the bride to her parents.
To my father, and my mother in heaven,\nI would not be here today\nif it weren't for the both of you.
Although my mother left me when I was young,\nher teachings and her love will always stay with me.
And, Dad.
When I was young, I wasn't able to sort\nout my feelings in the face of uncertainty,\nand there were other times when I was rebellious.
I will apologize now for those times.\nHowever, in time I came to understand\nhow you felt, Dad.
I'm so glad that you're my father.\nI truly mean that.
Once again, I express my gratitude to my family.\nI owe everything to my father, mother,\nand all my sisters.
Huh, me?
You're Ichika.
Ichika, the eldest quintuplet,\nis our kind big sister\nwho gently brings together our unique personalities.
As you may know,\nshe is a highly successful actress,\nand my role model.
Oh, is that so? You surprised me.
You are correct.
It brings back memories.\nYou gave me quite a hard time, you know.
People call you a cool beauty,\nbut I'm not fooled.
You're indulgent, dull,\nlazy, and you sleep too much.
Y-you didn't have to go THAT far...
But even so,\nyour determination to be strong appeared dazzling to me.\nYou're an amazing big sister.
What the? Why now, all of a sudden?
Hmph. While I've got the chance,\njust let me say what I want to say.
You're up next, Nino.
Nino, the second eldest, is a bit of a hot-tempered\ngirl, but she is a strong, strict older sister\nwho pushes everyone forward.
Her feminine charm is outstanding,\nand I still have much to learn from her.
That's right!
Good job, you got it, you got it!\nNext!
Why, at a time like this...
You just need to identify Yotsuba.
Your strength is in your compassion for others. \nEven though you act tough, I suppose it's because \nyou have so much love to give.
I couldn't find the answer at that time.\nI'm sorry.
It's okay...\nI...have no regrets...
I see.
Miku, yeah?
Yup.
Miku, the middle sister, strangely doesn't feel\nlike an older sister. She feels closer to that of\na dear friend, with whom I share the same standing.
That's why I deeply admire Miku,\nwho has achieved her own dreams.
Sometimes I get worried...\nAm I...doing okay...?
There's no need for me to even answer that.
You've always fought against your insecurities. \nAnd as a result you've accomplished so much and more.
Believe in yourself.\nYou are and always have been capable of great things.
I thought you'd say that.\nThank you, Futaro.
And finally, Itsuki.
Huh?
Huh?
But, I'm Yotsuba...
Itsuki is my one and only adorable little sister\nwhom I can be an older sister to. In reality though,\nshe is much more responsible than me.
When I was struggling with what to do,\nit was Itsuki who pushed me forward\nand gave me the courage.
Just kidding!
It was me, Itsuki!\nHow was that? Did I do well?
You...teasing me like this...
I have this chance! I'll say it now.
You're the reason my life went off track!\nIt was meeting you!
The root of all evil! The curry-loving yokai woman!
…! W-well, I never!
Before I met you, I could have never imagined\nthat there could be someone so lacking in\ndelicacy! You smart-ass! Compulsive kisser airhead!
I just don't think we'll ever get along, you and I!
Itsuki's...using her old way of speaking again.
Well, it's to be expected with Futaro around.
*sigh* Geez...
And, the last one is you, Yotsuba.\nYep. I got a perfect score.
Th-that was easy!
If my sisters weren't here,\nmy life would have been completely different.
There were times when I struggled with\nbeing a quintuplet. But for every moment of doubt,\nthere are hundreds of joyful memories that come to mind.
I've been with you for so long that I can't help\nbut have learned from you too. I may have been a tutor,\nbut I've learned a lot from you.
I feel really blessed to have been born as a sister\nto all of you quintuplets.
Meeting you quintuplets\nis one of my proudest achievements.
We might seem strange to others,\na little different from other families, but%p-2;――
I love my family, so much.
